# UC-22.56.14 · TSA-SD Aviation CIP Control Family 3 — DCS / BHS / AOC continuous-monitoring detection-coverage heat-map

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Every important airport and airline computer must have a 'guard' watching for cyber attacks. We map who has guards and who doesn't, every six hours.*

---

## Description

Reconciles aviation asset register with EDR/Falcon/Defender coverage; surfaces detection gaps.

## Value

TSA inspectors expect documented detection coverage for every aviation-critical asset.

## Implementation

(1) Populate `aviation_facility_asset_lookup`. (2) Populate `aviation_detection_risk_lookup`. (3) Schedule UC-22.56.14 every 6 hours. (4) Build the `TSA Aviation Detection Coverage` dashboard.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.56.14` archived every 6 hours to `audit_evidence` with `tsa_sd_clause="TSA-SD-1542-21-02-s3.3"`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable EDR on a DCS workstation. UC-22.56.14 MUST flag within 6 hours.

### Negative scenario

Re-enable EDR or pin compensating control. UC-22.56.14 MUST NOT escalate.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

Mirror UC-22.56.5 for aviation.

## Step 1 — Define aviation asset register

```ini
[aviation_facility_asset_lookup]
field.facility_id = string
field.asset_id = string
field.aviation_in_scope = string
field.aviation_zone = string
field.criticality = string
field.owner = string
```

## Step 2 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.56.14: TSA Aviation Detection Coverage]
cron_schedule = 9 */6 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.56.14,reg=TSA-Surface,clause=TSA-SD-1542-21-02-s3.3
alert.severity = 4
```

## Step 3 — Runbooks

**`detection_status=gap` on critical aviation asset**: investigate within 1 hour; deploy EDR or compensating control within 7 days.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

- **Vendor agent restrictions**: some BHS / DCS vendors prohibit EDR; document the prohibition + compensating control.

**TSA-specific failure modes**:
- **Joint-use airports**: airport authority + airlines + ground handlers may dispute responsibility; assign per-asset owner.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup aviation_facility_asset_lookup
| eval needs_detection = if(aviation_in_scope=true AND (criticality IN ("critical","high")), "true", "false")
| where needs_detection="true"
| join type=left asset_id [search (sourcetype="crowdstrike:falcon:event" OR sourcetype="mscs:wd:atp:device") | stats latest(_time) as last_telemetry by host_id | rename host_id as asset_id]
| lookup aviation_detection_risk_lookup asset_id AS asset_id OUTPUT risk_id risk_until
| eval detection_status = case(
    isnotnull(last_telemetry) AND last_telemetry > now()-86400, "covered",
    isnotnull(risk_id) AND risk_until > now(), "risk_accepted",
    1=1, "gap")
| where detection_status="gap"
| table facility_id asset_id aviation_zone criticality detection_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Coverage by facility. (Panel 2) Gap-trend (30 days). (Panel 3) Risk-accepted expiry. (Panel 4) Top-10 facilities with gaps.

## Known False Positives

1. **BHS embedded controllers** — many BHS PLCs lack EDR support; pin in risk-accepted with compensating network monitoring.
2. **Aerobridge HMI panels** — vendor-locked; pin with compensating Claroty CTD passive monitoring.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `aviation_detection_risk_lookup`.

## References

- [TSA SD-1542-21-02 §3.3 (continuous monitoring)](https://www.tsa.gov/sites/default/files/sd-1542-21-02.pdf)
- [NIST 800-137 — Information Security Continuous Monitoring](https://csrc.nist.gov/publications/detail/sp/800-137/final)
